title: Rosa oligantha
taxonomy: Rosa oligantha is classified as a species of rose, belonging to the genus Rosa. It is a member of the family Rosaceae and is native to China. Rosa oligantha is further categorized under the order Rosales and the class Magnoliopsida. The species is also known to be a part of the subclass Rosidae. This classification is based on the botanical system of plant taxonomy.
uses: Rosa oligantha is a species of rose, however information about its medicinal uses is limited. Further research is needed to determine any potential health benefits. Traditional uses of wild roses, including Rosa oligantha, have been reported for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Some species of roses have been used in herbal medicine to treat various conditions such as digestive issues and skin problems. The medicinal uses of Rosa oligantha specifically remain unclear and would require further scientific study.
